response.justification: Maria Rodriguez demonstrates strong full-stack development skills and a diverse technical background, but her experience with C++ and industrial automation is limited. While she has worked with high-performance systems and low-latency applications, her recent roles emphasize TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies, which are not the primary focus of this role. Her open-source contributions and mentorship align well with the behavioral competencies, but the technical fit for a C++-centric role is moderate.
strengths: Extensive experience with high-performance and low-latency systems (e.g., DLang at Sociomantic, C/C++ at National Instruments).
Strong background in mentorship, technical documentation, and cross-functional collaboration (e.g., workshops, open-source projects).
Diverse technical exposure, including event-driven architectures, REST APIs, and secure development practices.
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last notable role was at National Instruments in 2014–2015).
Primary expertise lies in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React), which are not core to this role.
No explicit mention of industrial automation, IoT protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards.
